actual.avg_kts_range: similar to 2026-03-05 — powered on 3.4m in open water
actual.swell_character: wind swell only — no ground swell — very very good wind swell quality

verdict.session_quality_desc: Great — strong wind swell, well-powered open water, excellent kite foil day
verdict.equipment_notes: Same kit as March 5 — 3.4m + 22m correct for E-direction open water.

observations.free_text: Conditions nearly identical to 2026-03-05. E-direction wind shadow present near shore — body drag required to launch. No ground swell today (unlike some previous sessions), but the wind swell was very very good — stronger and cleaner than expected. Once past the wind line, kite foiling was excellent. Wind swell only sessions in E conditions are proving to be underrated — the ENE wind swell at this period produces clean, consistent rollers that suit glide-style foil riding well. Second consecutive good open-water session despite near-shore access challenge. Pattern is solidifying: E-direction = wind shadow access challenge + clean wind swell beyond wind line.
anomalies: Zero ground swell — wind swell only, yet session quality was very high
Wind swell quality 'very very good' — stronger than March 5 equivalent
Consecutive E-direction days both producing quality open-water sessions
new_patterns: Wind swell alone (no ground swell) in E conditions can produce 'great day' kite foil quality
E-direction consecutive days: pattern repeats reliably — wind shadow + great open water
Wind-swell-only sessions should NOT be downgraded — can be excellent for kite foil
March 5-6 pattern: synoptic E-direction trade wind = wind shadow near-shore + quality wind swell offshore

calibration_notes.verdict_correction: Second consecutive confirmation: E-direction conditions should NOT auto-trigger NO-GO or MARGINAL. Correct call for experienced rider: GO with explicit wind-shadow access warning.
calibration_notes.wave_quality_correction: Wind-swell-only in E conditions = GOOD to VERY GOOD for kite foil. Ground swell not required for quality session. Remove ground-swell requirement from kite foil wave quality scoring.
calibration_notes.shadow_extent_correction: Wind shadow extent consistent — body drag required, but manageable. Not a cancellation condition.
calibration_notes.pattern_reinforcement: March 5 + March 6 are matching debriefs. E-direction synoptic easterly = repeatable pattern: (1) near-shore wind shadow, (2) body drag required, (3) excellent open-water wind swell, (4) 3.4m + 22m correct equipment. Confidence in this pattern is now HIGH.

differences: No ground swell today vs trace ENE ground swell on March 5
Wind swell quality rated 'very very good' today — possibly slightly better than March 5
